automotive-ev-battery.title: BYD May Take Over Stellantis & Other Legacy Auto Factories in Europe
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Chinese EV manufacturer BYD is reportedly in talks with Stellantis and other European automakers regarding the acquisition of underutilized factories across Europe. This development represents a significant shift in the global automotive landscape as Chinese companies pursue strategic expansion into the European market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Rivian opens R2 configurator: here are all the options and pricing
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Rivian has launched the online configurator for its mid-size R2 electric SUV, allowing reservation holders to customize their vehicles ahead of scheduled deliveries. The Performance trim starts at $57,990, positioning the R2 competitively against Tesla's Model Y, with the early configurator launch signaling production confidence at Rivian's Illinois facility.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Tesla Slashing Down Payments & Loan Terms in China Amidst Dropping Sales
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Tesla has reduced down payments and adjusted loan terms in China as the company faces declining sales in the world's largest EV market, with year-over-year sales down 10 percent in April and 15 percent for the first four months of 2026. The pricing adjustments reflect intensifying competition from domestic Chinese EV manufacturers.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Tesla finally reveals what happened in 17 'Robotaxi' crashes
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Tesla has unredacted crash narratives for 17 autonomous driving incidents previously filed with the NHTSA, providing the first detailed public account of each crash. The data indicates that most crashes were not attributable to the autonomous system itself, though the disclosure reveals some concerning incidents warranting further scrutiny.

automotive-ev-battery.title: World premiere: VW ID. Polo GTI – a compact electric sports car with major impact?
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Volkswagen has unveiled the ID. Polo GTI, bringing the iconic GTI nameplate into the electric era with 166 kW of power, an adaptive chassis, and heritage styling cues including a red stripe and tartan interior trim. Pre-sales are scheduled to begin in autumn at prices starting just below €39,000, representing VW's commitment to the performance EV segment.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Uber turns on Waymo as it pours $10B+ into owning robotaxi alternatives
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Uber is publicly criticizing its robotaxi partner Waymo while simultaneously investing over $10 billion to develop its own autonomous vehicle fleet in partnership with Rivian, Lucid, and Nuro. The strategic move reflects Uber's ambition to reduce dependence on external robotaxi providers while executives increasingly question Waymo's technology and deployment approach.

automotive-ev-battery.title: New Record For Electric Car Sales In Nordic Countries In April
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Electric vehicle sales in Nordic countries reached record levels in April 2026, with Norway and Denmark leading the regional market and Sweden and Finland also demonstrating strong performance. The results underscore the Nordic region's continued leadership in EV adoption and market maturity.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Volkswagen reveals the first electric GTI, a 222 hp EV hot hatch for $45,000
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Volkswagen has officially unveiled the ID. Polo GTI, a 222-horsepower electric hot hatch priced at approximately $45,000, marking the first electric iteration of the legendary GTI performance nameplate. The vehicle combines sporty performance credentials with modern EV technology to appeal to enthusiast consumers in the compact segment.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Tesla Full Self Driving (Supervised) Now Permitted in Belgium
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Tesla's Full Self Driving (Supervised) software has received regulatory approval for use in Belgium following prior deployment authorization in the Netherlands, with one test vehicle initially permitted to operate in Flanders. The expansion of FSD testing in Europe represents progress toward broader autonomous driving acceptance on the continent.

automotive-ev-battery.title: BMW is offering a 20% discount on EV charging at IONNA through September
automotive-ev-battery.summary: BMW and MINI drivers can access a 20 percent discount on all EV charging sessions through IONNA under the automaker's new preferred pricing program running through the end of September. The initiative aims to reduce charging costs and incentivize EV adoption among BMW and MINI brand owners.

world-news.title: Trump warns Taiwan against declaring independence, hours after summit with China's Xi
world-news.summary: US President Trump cautioned Taiwan against unilateral independence declarations during high-level discussions with Chinese leader Xi Jinping, urging both sides to reduce tensions over the self-governing island. The warning reflects ongoing geopolitical sensitivities surrounding Taiwan's status and US-China relations.

world-news.title: Trump and Xi conclude 'very successful' talks but few deals confirmed
world-news.summary: A two-day US-China summit between Trump and Xi concluded with ceremonial fanfare but yielded no significant trade agreements or policy breakthroughs. The talks represent a critical moment in US-China relations amid ongoing economic and strategic tensions.

world-news.title: Lebanon says six killed in Israeli strike as US announces ceasefire extension
world-news.summary: Six people were killed in an Israeli strike on Lebanon as the Trump-announced ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah was extended despite continued military exchanges between the parties. The renewed truce represents a fragile diplomatic effort to contain conflict in the volatile region.

world-news.title: Trump says 20-year nuclear programme suspension by Iran would be enough
world-news.summary: President Trump indicated that a 20-year suspension of Iran's nuclear program would satisfy US demands, conditional on Tehran demonstrating genuine commitment to verifiable nuclear fuel removal and uranium enrichment cessation. The statement signals potential flexibility in nuclear negotiations between Washington and Tehran.

world-news.title: Prisoner swap goes ahead as Kyiv mourns 24 killed in Russian strike on flats
world-news.summary: A prisoner exchange between Ukraine and Russia proceeded as Ukrainian officials reported 24 deaths from a Russian strike on residential buildings in Kyiv, including a 12-year-old girl. The dual developments underscore ongoing military conflict and humanitarian costs of the Russia-Ukraine war.

world-news.title: Iran war live: Lebanon, Israel extend truce; Tehran ready for more US talks
world-news.summary: Lebanon and Israel extended their ceasefire amid reporting of nearly 3,000 deaths since March, while Iran signaled willingness to continue diplomatic engagement with the United States. The developments reflect tentative regional de-escalation efforts alongside ongoing humanitarian toll.

world-news.title: Israel says it has killed Hamas commander in Gaza air strike
world-news.summary: Israeli forces reported killing Izz ad-Din al-Haddad, a senior Hamas military commander described as instrumental in planning the October 7 attacks, in a Gaza City strike. The operation represents continued Israeli military operations targeting Hamas leadership in the ongoing Gaza conflict.

world-news.title: Seven killed in Gaza on Nakba Day as Israel says it targets Hamas member
world-news.summary: Seven Palestinians, including three women and a child, were killed in Israeli strikes on Gaza City during Nakba Day commemorations as Israel continued targeting Hamas operatives. The incident reflects ongoing civilian casualties amid sustained Israeli military operations in Gaza.

world-news.title: Cuba hit by protests, blackouts amid oil shortage
world-news.summary: Cuba experienced widespread protests and electricity blackouts as an oil shortage triggered civil unrest in Havana and other areas. The energy crisis reflects broader economic challenges facing the Caribbean nation amid international sanctions and supply constraints.

world-news.title: New York Times defends journalist after Israel threatens to sue
world-news.summary: Israel's government announced legal action against New York Times reporting alleging that Israeli security officials sexually assaulted Palestinian detainees, with the publication defending its journalistic integrity and investigative methods. The dispute highlights tensions between press freedom and alleged state security concerns.

science-space.title: James Webb Space Telescope maps our universe's largest structure in unprecedented detail
science-space.summary: Astronomers using the James Webb Space Telescope have achieved unprecedented detailed mapping of the cosmic web, the largest structure in the universe. This breakthrough observation provides new insights into the distribution and organization of galaxies across cosmic scales.

science-space.title: We still can't see dark matter. But what if we can hear it?
science-space.summary: Scientists propose that colliding black holes may generate detectable signals from dark matter interactions, suggesting an innovative approach to studying this elusive cosmic component. This research could open new avenues for understanding dark matter through gravitational wave observations.

science-space.title: Mars orbiter captures striking images of 'chaos and craters' carved by ancient floods
science-space.summary: The ESA's Mars Express orbiter has captured dramatic new imagery revealing a rugged Martian landscape shaped by catastrophic flooding events billions of years ago. The images provide new evidence of Mars' dynamic geological past and the role of water in shaping its surface.

science-space.title: Hubble Sights Galaxy in Transition
science-space.summary: The NASA Hubble Space Telescope has captured an image of an enigmatic galaxy undergoing transition, displaying a bright center with hints of spiral structure obscured by reddish-brown dust clumps and filaments. This observation provides new insights into galactic morphology and evolution.

science-space.title: NASA Science, Cargo Launch on 34th SpaceX Resupply Mission to Station
science-space.summary: SpaceX's 34th commercial resupply mission successfully launched on a Falcon 9 rocket from Cape Canaveral, carrying nearly 6,500 pounds of scientific experiments and cargo to the International Space Station. The mission demonstrates continued progress in maintaining ISS operations and supporting ongoing research in microgravity.

science-space.title: Curiosity Shakes Loose a Pesky Rock
science-space.summary: After drilling a sample from a rock on Mars, NASA's Curiosity rover became inadvertently stuck with the entire rock attached to its robotic arm. Engineers successfully freed the rover through strategic repositioning and vibration techniques after several days of troubleshooting.

science-space.title: Bespoke DNA vaccine offers hope for treatment of notorious brain cancer
science-space.summary: Researchers have developed a personalized DNA vaccine approach showing promising results for treating an aggressive form of brain cancer. This breakthrough represents a significant advancement in precision oncology and immunotherapy strategies.

science-space.title: Picturing Earth in a New Light
science-space.summary: A comprehensive analysis has tracked changes in artificial light pollution at night across the globe, identifying regions where nighttime illumination has intensified and where it has diminished. This research has implications for understanding environmental change and human activity patterns.

science-space.title: Auroras over Australia look like sci-fi from space
science-space.summary: An astronaut aboard the International Space Station captured stunning imagery of brilliant green and pink auroras illuminating the Australian sky. The striking photograph showcases the dramatic natural light displays visible from orbital altitudes.

science-space.title: Briefing Chat: Hantavirus — what this outbreak reveals about the disease
science-space.summary: Nature provides an in-depth briefing on recent hantavirus outbreak developments and what these cases reveal about the disease's epidemiology and transmission patterns. The analysis offers important insights for public health preparedness and understanding zoonotic disease risks.

finance-markets.title: NextEra and Dominion in talks over tie-up to create $400bn US utility giant
finance-markets.summary: NextEra Energy and Dominion Energy are in discussions regarding a merger that would create a $400 billion utility conglomerate, driven by surging electricity demand from artificial intelligence data centres. The proposed tie-up, primarily structured as a stock deal, would represent a significant consolidation in the US energy sector at a time of unprecedented power consumption growth.

finance-markets.title: Global bonds tumble on fears of inflation shock from Iran war
finance-markets.summary: Global bond markets experienced significant losses as investors grew concerned about inflationary pressures stemming from escalating tensions in Iran, with US 30-year Treasury yields reaching their highest levels since 2007. The selloff reflects mounting fears that central banks may be forced to maintain or tighten monetary policy despite economic headwinds.

finance-markets.title: Stocks Post Worst Day Since March as Bonds Get Hit: Markets Wrap
finance-markets.summary: Global equity markets suffered their worst trading day since March as a bond market selloff derailed the ongoing stock rally, with investors increasingly concerned about central banks being compelled to tighten monetary policy amid persistently elevated oil prices. The market downturn reflects intensifying inflation concerns across financial markets.

finance-markets.title: Trump Fed nominees oppose terms of keeping Powell as temporary chair
finance-markets.summary: Trump's nominated Federal Reserve chair candidates have expressed opposition to the arrangement whereby incumbent Jerome Powell would remain as pro tempore chair until successor Kevin Warsh is sworn in, potentially as early as the following week. The disagreement signals potential friction over leadership transitions at the central bank during a period of significant monetary policy uncertainty.

finance-markets.title: Berkshire Sold $8 Billion of Chevron Shares as Prices Soared
finance-markets.summary: Berkshire Hathaway divested approximately $8 billion in Chevron Corporation shares during the first quarter as the oil company's stock reached record highs. The sale represents a significant reduction in the conglomerate's position in the energy sector amid elevated petroleum valuations.

finance-markets.title: Berkshire Amasses $2.6 Billion Stake in Delta Airlines
finance-markets.summary: Berkshire Hathaway established a $2.6 billion stake in Delta Air Lines during the first quarter, signalling a renewed investment in the airline industry despite the conglomerate's historical caution toward the sector. The move marks a significant capital deployment by the investment giant in commercial aviation equities.

finance-markets.title: Trump discloses hundreds of millions of dollars in trades in first quarter
finance-markets.summary: President Trump's financial disclosures reveal hundreds of millions of dollars in securities transactions during the first quarter, encompassing positions in technology, defence, and media companies including Nvidia, Palantir, Paramount, and Boeing. The extensive trading activity underscores the complexity of presidential financial holdings and potential conflicts of interest.

finance-markets.title: US federal prosecutors scrutinise BlackRock private credit fund
finance-markets.summary: Federal prosecutors are investigating valuation practices at BlackRock TCP Capital Corp, the asset management giant's private credit vehicle, as part of broader scrutiny of the private credit sector. The examination focuses on how the fund values its portfolio companies and reports performance metrics to investors.

finance-markets.title: Erock, a Power Systems Maker for Data Centers, Files for US IPO
finance-markets.summary: Erock Inc., a modular power systems manufacturer serving data centres and other industrial sectors, filed for a US initial public offering, disclosing growing revenues alongside widening losses. The IPO filing reflects investor interest in infrastructure providers supporting the expanding data centre industry.

finance-markets.title: Forbright Files for IPO Pitching Middle Market, Digital Banking
finance-markets.summary: Forbright Inc., a financial services platform specialising in middle-market lending and digital consumer banking, filed for an initial public offering while demonstrating growing customer deposits. The filing indicates robust growth in the digital banking and alternative lending sectors.

tech-ai.title: Power prices are up 76% on America's biggest grid, and a watchdog is pointing fingers
tech-ai.summary: Electricity prices have surged 76% on the United States' largest power grid, with regulators attributing the spike to AI-driven demand outpacing grid capacity. The situation underscores a fundamental infrastructure problem: the nation's power grid was not designed to support the energy demands of an AI-driven economy.

tech-ai.title: OpenAI launches ChatGPT for personal finance, will let you connect bank accounts
tech-ai.summary: OpenAI has introduced a new personal finance feature for ChatGPT that enables users to connect their bank accounts directly to the platform. The feature provides a unified dashboard displaying portfolio performance, spending patterns, subscription services, and upcoming payments.

tech-ai.title: OpenAI keeps shuffling its executives in bid to win AI agent battle
tech-ai.summary: OpenAI announced a major organizational restructuring with company president Greg Brockman leading all product development as the firm pivots toward AI agents. The reorganization consolidates multiple product divisions into a unified agentic platform, merging ChatGPT and Codex into a single experience.

tech-ai.title: A hotel check-in system left a million passports and driver's licenses open for anyone to see
tech-ai.summary: A hotel check-in system provider inadvertently exposed sensitive customer data including passport and driver's license information after misconfiguring its cloud storage to public access. The breach affected approximately one million individuals who had their identifying documents exposed without password protection.

tech-ai.title: YouTube is expanding its AI deepfake detection tool to all adult users
tech-ai.summary: YouTube is rolling out its AI-powered likeness detection feature to all users over 18, enabling individuals to scan for potential deepfakes of themselves on the platform. The technology uses facial recognition to identify lookalike content and allows users to request removal of matched videos.

tech-ai.title: Runway started by helping filmmakers — now it wants to beat Google at AI
tech-ai.summary: AI video-generation startup Runway is positioning itself as a contender in the broader AI market, betting that video generation capabilities represent a pathway to developing world models. The company argues that its position outside the tech giants gives it competitive advantages in innovation.

tech-ai.title: ArXiv will ban researchers who upload papers full of AI slop
tech-ai.summary: Academic preprint repository ArXiv is implementing enforcement measures against papers containing AI-generated content with insufficient human review, including hallucinated references and unedited language model outputs. Researchers submitting such papers will face one-year bans from the platform.

tech-ai.title: Tesla reveals two Robotaxi crashes involving teleoperators
tech-ai.summary: Newly unredacted crash reports disclose incidents involving Tesla's robotaxi service where teleoperators were operating vehicles at the time of collisions. The disclosures highlight ongoing challenges Tesla faces in scaling its autonomous vehicle operations safely.

tech-ai.title: Google updates its spam rules to include attempts to 'manipulate' AI
tech-ai.summary: Google has updated its search spam policy to classify attempts to manipulate AI systems—including efforts to influence AI Overview results—as prohibited spam techniques. The policy expansion addresses growing concerns about content optimization specifically designed to deceive generative AI models.

tech-ai.title: Silicon Valley's vacationland needs a new energy provider just as AI is driving prices up
tech-ai.summary: Lake Tahoe, a popular vacation destination for Silicon Valley technology executives, faces impending rate increases from its energy provider as artificial intelligence expansion drives up regional electricity demand. The situation illustrates the broader tension between technology industry growth and regional energy infrastructure capacity.

health-biotech.title: FDA drug center head Tracy Beth Høeg leaves as agency faces leadership vacuum
health-biotech.summary: Tracy Beth Høeg, acting director of the FDA's drug center, is departing the agency following Commissioner Marty Makary's recent resignation. The departures signal a significant leadership crisis at the FDA during a critical period for drug approvals and regulatory oversight.

health-biotech.title: A new Ebola outbreak is confirmed in a remote Congo province, with 65 deaths recorded
health-biotech.summary: A new Ebola outbreak has been confirmed in Congo's Ituri province, with 246 suspected cases and 65 confirmed deaths reported to date. The outbreak in the remote region presents significant public health challenges and raises concerns about global disease containment preparedness.

health-biotech.title: Takeda will pay $13.6 million to settle allegations it paid kickbacks to doctors
health-biotech.summary: Takeda Pharmaceuticals has agreed to pay $13.6 million to settle allegations that it provided kickbacks to physicians to prescribe one of its antidepressant medications. The settlement underscores ongoing regulatory scrutiny of pharmaceutical marketing practices and physician incentive arrangements.

health-biotech.title: Supreme Court preserves mail access for abortion pill
health-biotech.summary: The U.S. Supreme Court has ruled to continue allowing mifepristone to be distributed by mail, maintaining current safety protocols while litigation over the medication's accessibility remains ongoing. The decision represents a significant development in reproductive healthcare access and medication abortion availability.

health-biotech.title: Candel reports prostate cancer drug's long-term data ahead of FDA filing
health-biotech.summary: Candel Therapeutics has reported long-term follow-up data for its prostate cancer candidate scheduled for FDA submission later this year, with patient outcomes tracked 20 months post-treatment. The data may strengthen the company's regulatory submission and support approval prospects for the therapy.

health-biotech.title: Biopharma M&A maintains strength even as large deals wane
health-biotech.summary: The biopharma M&A landscape remains robust as major companies pursue multi-pronged acquisition strategies to address approaching patent cliffs, though mega-deals remain conspicuously absent. The activity reflects industry efforts to diversify portfolios and secure pipeline innovation amid significant patent expirations.

health-biotech.title: Amgen expands crackdown on what it says is misuse of 340B program
health-biotech.summary: Amgen has expanded its data requirements for pharmacies dispensing its drugs under the 340B federal drug discount program, citing concerns about fraud and program misuse. The action reflects heightened industry scrutiny of the 340B program's administration and potential compliance violations.

health-biotech.title: #ASGCT26: A Zillow-like marketplace for abandoned gene therapies goes live
health-biotech.summary: Two nonprofit organizations have launched a marketplace platform to help abandoned cell and gene therapies find new development partners or sponsors. The initiative addresses a critical gap in the advanced therapy landscape by connecting discontinued programs with potential acquirers.

health-biotech.title: M&A isn't just for big pharma. Mid-size companies are stepping up deal hunts
health-biotech.summary: Mid-cap American pharmaceutical companies and family-owned pharma groups are increasingly active in the M&A market, contributing to what appears to be a record-setting year for biopharma dealmaking. The diversification of acquirers reflects changing market dynamics and opportunities beyond traditional large pharmaceutical consolidation.

health-biotech.title: Opinion: First hantavirus, now Ebola: What two outbreaks reveal about global preparedness
health-biotech.summary: A commentary examines lessons from recent hantavirus and Ebola outbreaks, arguing that disease preparedness must be built through sustained efforts rather than crisis-driven responses. The piece underscores the importance of proactive global health infrastructure and early intervention capabilities.

crypto-web3.title: Crypto market structure bill clears key hurdle as ethics debate looms over floor vote
crypto-web3.summary: The Clarity Act advanced from the Senate Banking Committee with bipartisan support, positioning it for a potential full Senate vote within weeks. This represents a significant milestone in establishing comprehensive U.S. crypto market regulations.

crypto-web3.title: CME, ICE push U.S. regulators to scrutinize Hyperliquid over manipulation risks
crypto-web3.summary: Major exchanges CME Group and ICE have warned the CFTC and Capitol Hill officials that Hyperliquid's decentralized perpetual futures platform could enable market manipulation and sanctions evasion. The pressure signals growing regulatory scrutiny of decentralized derivatives platforms.

crypto-web3.title: U.S. House lawmakers who oversee the CFTC are urging Trump to fill the commission
crypto-web3.summary: Senior House lawmakers are calling on the Trump administration to appoint leadership to the Commodity Futures Trading Commission as the agency faces expanding responsibilities in policing U.S. crypto trading. The push underscores the need for bipartisan regulatory readiness in the sector.

crypto-web3.title: Saudi Arabia is tokenizing its multi-trillion dollar economy to protect its wealth from global shocks
crypto-web3.summary: Saudi Arabia is pursuing a major tokenization initiative with $12.5 billion in mandates secured by droppRWA to bring real estate and other assets onchain. The strategy signals institutional-scale adoption of blockchain technology for wealth preservation by sovereign entities.

crypto-web3.title: Lombard joins LayerZero exodus as $4 billion in assets switch to Chainlink's bridge
crypto-web3.summary: Following the Kelp DAO exploit that drained $292 million from its LayerZero-powered bridge, significant capital is migrating to Chainlink's cross-chain infrastructure. The shift reflects heightened security concerns surrounding LayerZero's bridge technology.

crypto-web3.title: Kraken parent Payward cuts 150 staff, streamlining business ahead of planned IPO
crypto-web3.summary: Payward is executing a workforce reduction of 150 employees while pursuing fresh funding at a $20 billion valuation and accelerating acquisitions in preparation for a public listing. The restructuring reflects strategic positioning before going public.

crypto-web3.title: Crypto market structure bill still faces significant hurdles despite Senate committee win: analysts
crypto-web3.summary: Analysts warn that the Clarity Act faces meaningful obstacles moving forward, with TD Cowen raising passage probability to 40% and Benchmark noting the bill needs additional Democratic support. The committee approval does not guarantee Senate floor passage.

crypto-web3.title: Poland passes MiCA crypto bill as $96 million Zondacrypto probe deepens: report
crypto-web3.summary: Poland adopted the Markets in Crypto Assets (MiCA) regulation while prosecutors investigate $96 million in losses from the collapsed Zondacrypto exchange. The dual developments underscore Europe's regulatory momentum and enforcement against crypto fraud.

crypto-web3.title: IREN closes $3 billion convertible notes offering as Bitcoin miner's AI infrastructure push accelerates
crypto-web3.summary: Bitcoin miner IREN completed a $3 billion convertible notes offering at a 1% coupon to fund expansion of AI cloud infrastructure following recent deals with Nvidia and Microsoft. The funding demonstrates crypto miners' strategic pivot toward artificial intelligence services.

crypto-web3.title: It's a 'new era' for crypto users who 'trade anything and everything' as Jito goes consumer, CEO Bruder says
crypto-web3.summary: Jito founder Lucas Bruder indicated that a new class of retail users is emerging onchain with appetite to trade diverse asset classes. The expansion into consumer offerings reflects growing mainstream interest in decentralized trading infrastructure.

cybersecurity.title: CISA Adds Cisco SD-WAN CVE-2026-20182 to KEV After Admin Access Exploits
cybersecurity.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency added a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, requiring Federal agencies to remediate by May 17, 2026. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20182, is being actively exploited in the wild and poses significant risk to government infrastructure.

cybersecurity.title: On-Prem Microsoft Exchange Server CVE-2026-42897 Exploited via Crafted Email
cybersecurity.summary: Microsoft disclosed a critical security vulnerability in on-premise Exchange Server versions that is undergoing active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability, CVE-2026-42897 with a CVSS score of 8.1, stems from a cross-site scripting flaw that enables spoofing attacks through crafted emails.

cybersecurity.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
cybersecurity.summary: Major software vendors including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla, and Oracle released near-record volumes of security patches in May 2026, driven in part by AI systems' effectiveness at identifying vulnerabilities in human-created code. The accelerated patch releases reflect the growing sophistication of AI-powered vulnerability discovery tools alongside continued security threats.

cybersecurity.title: Turla Turns Kazuar Backdoor Into Modular P2P Botnet for Persistent Access
cybersecurity.summary: The Russian state-sponsored hacking group Turla, assessed to be affiliated with Russia's Federal Security Service, has transformed its Kazuar backdoor into a modular peer-to-peer botnet designed for stealth and persistent access to compromised systems. This advancement indicates heightened capabilities for maintaining long-term unauthorized access to victim networks.

cybersecurity.title: 'CanisterWorm' Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
cybersecurity.summary: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group has released a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and targets systems configured with Iran's time zone or Farsi language settings. The campaign represents an attempt to merge cybercriminal extortion tactics with geopolitical targeting related to the Iran conflict.

cybersecurity.title: Four OpenClaw Flaws Enable Data Theft, Privilege Escalation, and Persistence
cybersecurity.summary: Cybersecurity researchers disclosed four chained vulnerabilities in OpenClaw, collectively dubbed Claw Chain, that allow attackers to establish initial compromise, exfiltrate sensitive data, and deploy persistent backdoors. The vulnerability set demonstrates how multiple flaws can be weaponized in sequence to achieve complete system compromise.

cybersecurity.title: 'FrostyNeighbor' APT Carefully Targets Govt Orgs in Poland, Ukraine
cybersecurity.summary: A Belarussian nation-state threat group tracked as FrostyNeighbor is conducting targeted campaigns against government organizations in Poland and Ukraine using uniquely fingerprinted spear-phishing payloads. The sophisticated targeting approach indicates espionage objectives and careful victim profiling by the state-sponsored attackers.

cybersecurity.title: Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
cybersecurity.summary: A Brazilian cybersecurity firm specializing in DDoS protection has been implicated in enabling botnet attacks against competing network operators, according to investigative reporting. The company's leadership attributed the malicious activity to a security breach, suggesting competitors may have compromised their infrastructure to damage the firm's reputation.

cybersecurity.title: TanStack Supply Chain Attack Hits Two OpenAI Employee Devices, Forces macOS Updates
cybersecurity.summary: OpenAI disclosed that two employee devices were compromised through the Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain attack targeting TanStack, though the company confirmed no user data, production systems, or intellectual property were affected. The incident highlights the ongoing threat of supply chain compromises impacting major AI companies.

cybersecurity.title: Funnel Builder WordPress plugin bug exploited to steal credit cards
cybersecurity.summary: A critical vulnerability in the Funnel Builder WordPress plugin is being actively exploited to inject malicious JavaScript code into WooCommerce checkout pages for credit card theft. The widespread use of the affected plugin puts thousands of e-commerce sites at immediate risk of payment fraud.

startups-vc.title: Kevin Hartz's A* just closed its third fund with $450M
startups-vc.summary: A*, the generalist venture firm founded by Kevin Hartz, has closed its third fund with $450 million in capital commitments. The fund will deploy capital across AI applications, fintech, healthcare, and security with average check sizes between $3 million and $5 million, targeting at least 30 startup investments.

startups-vc.title: The Week's 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: Anduril Leads Varied Lineup Of Large Deals
startups-vc.summary: Defense technology unicorn Anduril Industries secured $5 billion in funding, leading a week of substantial capital deployment across physical-world focused companies. The fundraising landscape also featured significant rounds for robotics, space technology, biotech, and data infrastructure companies.

startups-vc.title: Meridian Ventures launched a $35M fund with a focus on MBA-deferred founders
startups-vc.summary: Meridian Ventures has launched a $35 million fund dedicated to backing enterprise technology founders who have deferred or skipped traditional MBA paths. The firm maintains a sector-agnostic approach with investments already deployed across fintech, logistics, healthcare, and AI applications.

startups-vc.title: Congratulations to EquipmentShare on Going Public
startups-vc.summary: EquipmentShare, a Y Combinator-backed construction technology platform that has expanded to 373 locations nationwide, has completed its initial public offering. Founded by contractors for contractors, the company has built an operating system addressing a critical gap in the construction industry.

startups-vc.title: Figma Beats, Raises and Accelerates! Boom! Stock Rockets 12%! But … It Still Trades for Less than 10X ARR
startups-vc.summary: Figma delivered strong first-quarter earnings with accelerating revenue growth, a significant net dollar retention rate improvement, and validated AI monetization strategies that exceeded market expectations. Despite the positive performance and 12% stock price increase, the design platform company trades below 10x annual recurring revenue multiples.

startups-vc.title: European AI Funding Is Growing. Will That Boost The Region's Startup Scene?
startups-vc.summary: European venture capital allocation toward AI-focused companies is increasing substantially in 2026, including funding for three newly established frontier model companies and startups across diverse AI-centric sectors. This funding trend signals growing investor confidence in Europe's AI ecosystem development and potential competitive positioning.

startups-vc.title: Harshita Arora Joins YC as General Partner
startups-vc.summary: Y Combinator has appointed Harshita Arora as a General Partner, bringing extensive fintech and infrastructure expertise alongside a founder's perspective from building companies since her teenage years. Arora's addition strengthens YC's investment capabilities in financial technology and core infrastructure domains.

startups-vc.title: 5 Interesting Startup Deals You May Have Missed: A Law Firm Operating System, Building Defense Tech Near The Battlefield, And Cell-Based Milk
startups-vc.summary: Crunchbase identified five overlooked startup deals focused on solving real-world problems, including a law firm operating system platform, a defense technology company manufacturing containerized equipment platforms, and a cell-based milk production startup. These ventures represent a broader market shift toward physical-world applications and infrastructure innovation.

startups-vc.title: The OpenAI trial wraps up, and the Musk founder machine keeps spinning
startups-vc.summary: The legal dispute between Elon Musk and Sam Altman over OpenAI has concluded with final arguments centered on questions of trust and governance in artificial intelligence leadership. The trial's resolution comes as Musk's SpaceX prepares for what could be one of the largest initial public offerings in American history.

startups-vc.title: The Crunchbase Tech Layoffs Tracker
startups-vc.summary: Tech sector employment continues to contract with over 127,000 workers laid off at U.S.-based technology companies in 2025, with reductions continuing into 2026. The ongoing layoff trend reflects industry consolidation and shifting capital allocation priorities across the startup ecosystem.

energy-climate.title: DeBriefed 15 April 2026: Trump-Xi talk energy | 'Supercharged' El Niño | India's first 'heat lounges'
energy-climate.summary: Carbon Brief's weekly digest covers major developments in climate and energy policy, including discussions between Trump and Xi on energy matters, intensified El Niño conditions affecting global climate patterns, and India's innovative adaptation measures to extreme heat through community heat lounges.

energy-climate.title: Analysis: UK no longer top UN Green Climate Fund donor after latest aid cut
energy-climate.summary: The United Kingdom has relinquished its position as the leading contributor to the UN's Green Climate Fund following recent reductions in climate finance commitments, signaling a shift in global climate funding leadership.

energy-climate.title: Fraunhofer ISE achieves 31.3% record solar-to-hydrogen efficiency in CPV electrolysis
energy-climate.summary: German researchers at Fraunhofer ISE have achieved a record 31.3% solar-to-hydrogen conversion efficiency using micro-concentrator photovoltaics coupled with proton exchange membrane electrolysis, advancing the viability of green hydrogen production technology.

energy-climate.title: Brazil deploys 4.4 GW of solar in Q1
energy-climate.summary: Brazil expanded its solar capacity by 4.4 gigawatts in the first quarter of 2026, comprising 2.3 GW of utility-scale and 2.2 GW of distributed generation, demonstrating sustained momentum in Latin America's renewable energy transition.

energy-climate.title: UK grants development consent for offshore wind farms
energy-climate.summary: The United Kingdom government has approved development consent for the Dogger Bank South and North Falls offshore wind projects, advancing the nation's renewable energy infrastructure and carbon reduction goals.

energy-climate.title: The Hydrogen Stream: Ireland's LCOH on par with Morocco, Brazil
energy-climate.summary: Fraunhofer ISE and Irish energy company ESB have demonstrated that Ireland possesses competitive advantages for green hydrogen production with levelized costs comparable to Morocco, South Africa, and Brazil, supported by robust wind resources and favorable policy conditions.

energy-climate.title: Cropped 6 May 2026: Forest loss falls | Deforestation regulations | Saving 'India's Galapagos'
energy-climate.summary: Carbon Brief's digest on climate and land-use topics reports declining forest loss rates, emerging deforestation regulations, and conservation efforts for biodiverse regions in India, highlighting progress in natural capital protection.

energy-climate.title: Argentina commissions 360 MW solar farm
energy-climate.summary: Argentina has brought online a $220 million solar project with 305 MW capacity in Mendoza province, expanding the nation's renewable energy portfolio in one of South America's sunniest regions.

energy-climate.title: JinkoSolar, Masdar sign 2GW PV module supply agreement for RTC project
energy-climate.summary: Chinese solar manufacturer JinkoSolar and Abu Dhabi's Masdar have executed a supply agreement for 2 gigawatts of Tiger Neo photovoltaic modules for the RTC renewable energy project in the United Arab Emirates.

energy-climate.title: Solar PPAs raise Spanish spot prices, not suppress them
energy-climate.summary: A peer-reviewed analysis of Spain's electricity market reveals that physical bilateral contracts from wind and solar installations increase wholesale power prices during high-penetration phases, reversing earlier price-suppression effects.

asia-pacific.title: Google’s YouTube, Snap settle first-of-its-kind school social media suit ahead of trial
asia-pacific.summary:
Google’s YouTube and Snap reached agreements to settle the first lawsuit headed to trial over claims that addiction to top social media platforms has disrupted learning and pushed public schools to spend massive sums fighting a mental health crisis, according to court filings.
TikTok and Meta Platforms were also sued by the rural Kentucky school district that brought the case, which is set to go to trial on June 12 in federal court in Oakland, California.
The trial will serve as a test case for.

asia-pacific.title: Hong Kong to test first flying cars carrying heavy cargo within 6 months
asia-pacific.summary:
Trial flights for Hong Kong’s first two-tonne electric vertical take-off and landing (eVTOL) aircraft could take place within the next six months, initially to transport heavy construction materials to remote sites in the New Territories.
Infrastructure consultancy AECOM said once aviation safety for cargo logistics was proved, the potential next step was ecotourism, providing an alternative sustainable option for Victoria Harbour aerial sightseeing tours.
The trial was part of a recently...

asia-pacific.title: China couple make wedding memorable by hosting wrestling match, with loser facing housework
asia-pacific.summary:
A couple in southwestern China turned their wedding into a professional wrestling showdown, with the loser agreeing to do the housework for life.
Unlike conventional competitive sports, professional wrestling is a choreographed combat performance.
The groom, He Yinsheng, a professional wrestler from Zunyi in Guizhou province, staged the unusual celebration with his wife at a local hotel in early May.
In place of a traditional wedding stage, the couple installed a wrestling ring, with a giant...

asia-pacific.title: Iran war fallout triggers massive biofuel shift across Asia
asia-pacific.summary:
Taxi driver Ravi Ranjan, who lives with his wife and child in New Delhi, said shipping disruptions caused by the Iran war had forced him to pay higher prices for cooking fuel at a time when India’s prime minister was also urging residents to reduce driving and travel.
It was all hitting his bottom line, Ranjan said, as he was paying three times as much for liquid petroleum gas after facing delays on delivery of the cooking fuel.
“I used to get a cylinder of LPG for 1,000 rupees (US$10), now I...

asia-pacific.title: Beyond bamboo: Vietnam’s To Lam mounts a diplomacy offensive
asia-pacific.summary:
Vietnam’s To Lam is a man in a hurry. Since claiming the nation’s top job, its most powerful leader in decades has been in near-constant motion, pressing flesh and signing deals in Beijing, Washington, Pyongyang and Moscow.
His itinerary, which also included stops in New Delhi, Helsinki, Paris, London and several Southeast Asian capitals, reads less like a diplomatic calendar than a world tour – and analysts say that is precisely the point.
“Where previous leaders practised a restrained,...

asia-pacific.title: Trump leaves China after much pomp and pageantry, but little to show for it
asia-pacific.summary:
As US President Donald Trump landed back in Washington on Friday after his two-day summit in Beijing with Chinese President Xi Jinping, a quick US assessment on one of the most consequential diplomatic visits of Trump’s presidency was: impressive optics, trademark flawless Chinese hosting, but disappointing concrete achievements, according to analysts, industry trade groups and former US officials.
“The economic deliverables coming out of the Trump-Xi meeting are way below expectations. In light

asia-pacific.title: Trump and his CEOs want China’s business – but has Asia moved on?
asia-pacific.summary:
US President Donald Trump’s landmark visit to China comes as the US-Iran war disrupts global energy supplies, fuels economic uncertainty and adds fresh strain to Washington-Beijing ties. In the latest instalment of a series examining how rivalry, interdependence and geopolitical crises are reshaping the relationship between the two powers, we explore the massive upswing in capital expenditures across Asia that is driving a broad shift in economic power.
When US President Donald Trump made his...

asia-pacific.title: Travolta gets surprise Cannes prize: ‘beyond the Oscar’
asia-pacific.summary:
Hollywood star John Travolta was given a surprise lifetime achievement award at the Cannes Film Festival Friday as he premiered the first movie he has ever directed.
The man who became an icon overnight with Saturday Night Fever was visibly moved as he accepted the honorary Palme d’Or before the screening of Propeller One-Way Night Coach, which is based on a book about his first experience in an airliner.
“I just can’t believe it. This is beyond the Oscar, really,” he said as he accepted the...

asia-pacific.title: How to deal with this ‘very Chinese time’ in Western lives
asia-pacific.summary:
What comes to mind when you think of China, Japan and South Korea?
This was a question posed in a second-year sociology seminar during my time at Bristol, and it has stayed with me ever since. We were split into three groups and given 10 minutes to create posters capturing our immediate associations.
Japan came first. Sketches of anime, sushi platters and temples appeared. My classmates spoke with enthusiasm, drawing on travel memories or aspirations to visit. A country that, less than a century

asia-pacific.title: Canada takes key step towards new oil pipeline to serve Asia markets
asia-pacific.summary:
Prime Minister Mark Carney and the leader of Canada’s oil-rich Alberta province took a major step on Friday towards building an oil pipeline that could substantially increase crude exports to Asia.
Expanding overseas energy exports has emerged as a key part of Carney’s strategy to reduce Canada’s economic reliance on the US, but plans for a new pipeline are facing stiff resistance over environmental concerns.
Alberta’s conservative Premier Danielle Smith was a relentless critic of Carney’s...
